messages.content:
CVE-2024-46981 is a critical vulnerability in Redis, an open source, in-memory database. This vulnerability arises from improper memory handling, allowing an authenticated user to execute remote code through a specially crafted Lua script by manipulating the garbage collector. Identified under CWE-416, this flaw is associated with potentially executing unintended operations or causing a program crash due to referencing memory after it has been freed.

The affected versions of Redis range from 6.2.0 up to 6.2.16, 7.2.0 up to 7.2.6, and 7.4.0 up to 7.4.1. Official fixes have been implemented in versions 7.4.2, 7.2.7, and 6.2.17.

Mitigation techniques include updating to the patched versions or using Access Control Lists (ACL) to restrict the execution of Lua scripts by blocking EVAL and EVALSHA commands. Additionally, the vulnerability is linked to the MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1190, highlighting its potential exploitation through remote code execution means.

Given its critical severity, organizations are urged to apply updates promptly or restrict script execution as interim safeguarding measures.
